summary: Frames timely large-load interconnection as central to the AI race and national security, and urges FERC to prioritize the reforms it can implement fastest — ones that maximize existing infrastructure and reward flexibility to minimize grid strain.
key_points: Prioritize the quickest-to-implement reforms over a slow comprehensive rule.
Maximize use of existing transmission (alternative transmission technologies, flexible/curtailable operation) before building new upgrades.
Speed to power is a national-security and economic-competitiveness imperative.
Supports large loads bearing the costs they cause, paired with efficient, predictable study processes.

quotes.text: Google urges the Commission to promptly and pragmatically prioritize the reforms that can be implemented most quickly and that will incentivize digital and energy infrastructure development in a manner that maximizes use of the existing infrastructure and minimizes strain on the grid.
